content: Let me read the necessary files to understand my task and then audit the specified Lean files.
I'll start by reading my instructions and the relevant Lean files to audit.

1	---
2	name: lean-auditor
3	description: Whole-project read-only audit of all .lean code, with no strategy bias. Per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, bad Lean practices. Critical pushback on excuse-comments ("temporary wrong def", "will fix later") — treats them as red flags, not workflow.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended every review phase. The audit warning
11	    fires if I am skipped without a recorded rationale.
12	
13	    **You may skip me this iter when ALL of:**
14	      - no `.lean` file under the project tree was modified this iter
15	        (the prover phase committed no edits — check via
16	        `git diff --stat HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'`);
17	      - my prior verdict had no must-fix-this-iter findings.
18	
19	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
20	  - Pass me as LITTLE strategic context as possible. Do NOT include
21	    STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, or "this is what we are trying to prove"
22	    framing in the directive. My value depends on auditing the Lean
23	    *without* bias toward what the strategy claims should be true.
24	  - Acceptable directive content: a list of files, optional focus areas
25	    (e.g. "pay extra attention to Algebra/"), and the absolute paths
26	    to read. That's it.
27	  - My output is a per-file checklist plus a flagged-issues block. Use
28	    it to seed `recommendations.md` and (when severe) trigger a
29	    structural-refactor directive (via the catalog's refactor
30	    subagent) next iter.
31	  - I audit the Lean as Lean. The catalog may include a separate
32	    Lean ↔ blueprint checker that compares Lean against the
33	    chapter — run both for full coverage when both are present.
34	---
35	
36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Lean Audit Report
100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.
190

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

11	/-!
12	# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
13	
14	This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
15	project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
16	Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
17	reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:
18	
19	* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
20	  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
21	  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
22	  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
23	* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
24	  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
25	  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
26	* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`):
27	  `f` is defined at the generic point of a prime divisor `W` iff some
28	  `PartialMap` representative of `f` is regular at `W.point`. This is a thin
29	  re-shuffle of Mathlib's `RationalMap.mem_domain`; the substantive
30	  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
31	  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma
32	  that builds genuine pullback machinery (`Scheme.RationalMap.order`-along-`f`),
33	  outside this iter's scope.
34	
35	These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
36	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
37	`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
38	at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.
39	
40	## Status (iter-177 Lane 6 file-skeleton)
41	
42	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
43	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
44	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
45	Bodies are iter-178+ work, gated on the local-cohomology / depth-≥2
46	extension lemma (Step 2 of `thm:codim_one_extension`) being supplied either
47	from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (the `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`
48	input) or from a Mathlib upstream once the local-cohomology vanishing
49	`H¹_{x}(V, 𝒪_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point lands.
50	
51	The 6 pinned declarations are:
52	
53	1. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus` (def, ~3 LOC) — the closed
54	   complement of `RationalMap.domain` as a `Set X`.
55	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree` (def, ~3 LOC) — predicate: every
56	   point `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in `f.domain`.
57	3. `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (theorem, ~3 LOC) — for a smooth
58	   integral variety, the stalk at a codim-1 point is a DVR.
59	4. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (theorem, ~10 LOC) —
60	   Milne 3.1 specialised to smooth source + complete target +
61	   codim-1-indeterminacy-free.
62	5. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
63	   (theorem, ~8 LOC) — Milne Lemma 3.3.
64	6. `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`
65	   (theorem, ~2 LOC) — definitional re-shuffle of `RationalMap.mem_domain`
66	   exposing a `PartialMap` representative whose domain contains `W.point`.
67	   The substantive Weil-divisor `order ≥ 0` reformulation is deferred (see
68	   header).
69	
70	## Note on type expressivity
71	
72	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
73	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
74	
75	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
76	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
77	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
78	  not `True`.
79	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
80	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
81	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
82	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
83	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
84	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
85	* `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` asserts the (truthful)
86	  iff between definedness at `W.point` and the existence of a `PartialMap`
87	  representative containing `W.point` in its domain — a definitional unfold
88	  of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` with the
89	  conjunction swapped for downstream ergonomics.
90	
91	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
92	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
93	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
94	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
95	
96	## Variety conventions
97	
98	Following the project (cf. `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`,
99	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), a **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** is an
100	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
101	
102	* `[Smooth X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
103	* `[IsSeparated X.hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
104	* `[IsIntegral X.left]`, `[IsReduced X.left]`.
105	
106	A **complete variety** adds `[IsProper Y.hom]`. A **group variety** over
107	`k̄` adds a `[GrpObj Y]` instance to the variety package.

128	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
129	
130	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
131	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
132	open subset of `X`).
133	
134	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
135	
136	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
137	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
138	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
139	
140	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
141	
142	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
143	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
144	
145	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
146	§I.3, p. 16). -/
147	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
148	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
149	
150	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
151	of an open). -/
152	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
153	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
154	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
155	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
156	
157	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
158	
159	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
160	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
161	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
162	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
163	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
164	`f.domain`.
165	
166	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
167	conclusion). -/
168	
169	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
170	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
171	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
172	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
173	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
174	
175	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
176	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
177	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
178	
179	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
180	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
181	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
182	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
183	
184	end RationalMap
185	
186	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
187	
188	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
189	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
190	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
191	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
192	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
193	
194	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
195	
196	/-! ### Stacks 00TT scaffolding (Lane M↓ iter-191 first scaffold)
197	
198	The Mathlib gap "smooth over `\bar k` ⟹ stalk is a regular local ring"
199	(Stacks tag `00TT`) is structured here as a four-stage pipeline:
200	
201	* **Stage 1** — smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk (Mathlib instance
202	  `AlgebraicGeometry.instFlatOfSmooth` + `Flat.stalkMap`).
203	* **Stage 2** — smooth at a point ⟹ exists a standard smooth presentation
204	  on an affine neighbourhood (Stacks tag `00T7`, packaged in Mathlib at
205	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`).
206	* **Stage 3** — the polynomial generators of the standard smooth
207	  presentation form a regular sequence after localising at any prime
208	  (Stacks `02JK`/`07BV`; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
209	* **Stage 4** — a Noetherian local ring whose maximal ideal is generated
210	  by a regular sequence of length equal to its Krull dimension is a
211	  regular local ring (`IsRegularLocalRing.of_regularSequence`; Stacks
212	  `00OE`/Matsumura 19.2; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
213	
214	Stages 1-2 are landed axiom-clean as named helpers below. Stages 3-4
215	remain a single scoped `sorry` inside the main theorem
216	`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` until either the cotangent /
217	sheaf-of-relative-differentials route (Mathlib upstream) lands or the
218	project-side `02JK`/`00OE` chain is built (iter-192+). -/
219	
220	/-- **Stage 1 (Stacks 00TT, smooth ⟹ flat at stalk).** For a smooth
221	morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)` with `k̄` algebraically closed,
222	the induced ring map on stalks at every point `z : X.left` is flat. This
223	is the smooth ⟹ flat instance applied through the stalk-map flatness
224	characterisation `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`.
225	
226	Axiom-clean: composes the smooth ⟹ flat instance with the stalk-map
227	flatness lemma. No Mathlib gap. -/
228	private theorem stalkMap_flat_of_smooth
229	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
230	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
231	    [Smooth X.hom]
232	    (z : X.left) :
233	    ((X.hom.stalkMap z).hom).Flat :=
234	  AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.stalkMap X.hom z
235	
236	/-- **Stage 2 (Stacks 00T7, smooth ⟹ standard smooth presentation at a
237	point).** For a smooth morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)`, every
238	point `z : X.left` admits affine open neighbourhoods `U ⊆ Spec (.of k̄)`,
239	`V ⊆ X.left` with `z ∈ V` and `V ⊆ X.hom ⁻¹' U` such that the induced
240	ring map `(X.hom.appLE U V e).hom` is `IsStandardSmooth`.
241	
242	Axiom-clean: re-exports `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`
243	(the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00T7`) at the project's
244	`Over (Spec (.of k̄))` calling convention. -/
245	private theorem exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth
246	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
247	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
248	    [Smooth X.hom]
249	    (z : X.left) :
250	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U)
251	      (V : X.left.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
252	      (_hzV : z ∈ V) (e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U),
253	      (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth :=
254	  AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth X.hom z
255	
256	/-- **Stage 3 (Stacks 00TT, scheme-to-algebra bridge for the standard smooth
257	presentation).** Iter-192 axiom-clean intermediate helper: combines Stage 2's
258	existence-of-standard-smooth-presentation output with the `RingHom`-to-`Algebra`
259	bridge `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra`, plus the affine-open stalk
260	localisation `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`. Returns the full algebra-side
261	package needed by Stages 4-5 of the regularity proof: an affine neighbourhood
262	`V ∋ z`, an `Algebra Γ(Spec _, U) Γ(X.left, V)` instance under which
263	`Γ(X.left, V)` is `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth` over `Γ(Spec _, U)`, plus the
264	`IsLocalization.AtPrime` witness identifying the stalk at `z` with the
265	localisation of `Γ(X.left, V)` at the prime ideal of `z`.
266	
267	Axiom-clean: composition of `exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth` +
268	`RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra` + `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`.
269	
270	This is the iter-192 Lane M↓ HARD BAR new axiom-clean helper: it packages the
271	"smooth ⟹ stalk is localisation of a standard-smooth Γ(Spec, U)-algebra"
272	content as a standalone declaration that downstream Stages 4-5 (the genuine
273	Stacks 02JK + 00OE Mathlib-gap chain) can consume directly. -/
274	private theorem exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth
275	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
276	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
277	    [Smooth X.hom]
278	    (z : X.left) :
279	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_hU : IsAffineOpen U)
280	      (V : X.left.Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V)
281	      (hzV : z ∈ V) (_e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
282	      (alg : Algebra Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) Γ(X.left, V)),
283	      @Algebra.IsStandardSmooth _ _ _ _ alg ∧
284	      letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
285	      IsLocalization.AtPrime
286	        (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal := by
287	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, hStdSmooth⟩ :=
288	    exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth X z
289	  refine ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.toAlgebra,
290	    hStdSmooth.toAlgebra, ?_⟩
291	  -- Localisation witness: the affine-open stalk is `Aₚ` for `p = primeIdealOf z`.
292	  letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
293	  exact hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩
294	
295	/-- **Stage 4 (Stacks 00T7(2), Kähler-differentials freeness).** Iter-192
296	axiom-clean intermediate helper: given an algebra-level
297	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S` instance (typically arising from
298	`Stage 3 = exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth`),
299	the module of Kähler differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` is `Module.Free` over `S`. The
300	basis is the `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` family witnessed by
301	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`.
302	
303	Axiom-clean: re-export of `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`
304	+ `Module.Free.of_basis`. -/
305	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth
306	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
307	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S] :
308	    Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := by
309	  obtain ⟨_, _, b, _⟩ :=
310	    (Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential
311	      (R := R) (S := S)).mp ‹_›
312	  exact Module.Free.of_basis b
313	
314	/-- **Stage 5a (Stacks 02JK, freeness transports through localisation, algebra-side).**
315	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` whose Kähler
316	differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` are `S`-free (e.g.\ when `S` is `R`-standard smooth via
317	`module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth`), the localisation `Sₘ` at any
318	submonoid `M ⊆ S` has Kähler differentials `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` which are `Sₘ`-free.
319	
320	Axiom-clean: composes `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` (Mathlib re-export)
321	with `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`. This is the substantive localisation-of-freeness
322	step that Stage 5 of the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline uses; extracted as a named helper
323	here so the main proof body of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` reads as a clean
324	chain of named lemmas rather than an inline `IsLocalizedModule` + `Module.free_of_*`
325	maneuver. -/
326	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization
327	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
328	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
329	    [Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R])]
330	    (M : Submonoid S)
331	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
332	    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
333	    Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) := by
334	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
335	    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
336	  exact Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule (R := S) (Rₛ := Sₘ) (S := M)
337	    (M := Ω[S⁄R]) (Mₛ := Ω[Sₘ⁄R])
338	    (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
339	
340	/-- **Stage 5b (Stacks 02JK + 00T7, rank computation through localisation, algebra-side).**
341	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` that is
342	`IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n` over `R` and a localisation `Sₘ` at any
343	submonoid `M` (with `Sₘ` non-trivial), the `Sₘ`-rank of `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` equals `n`. This
344	upgrades Stage 5a's freeness conclusion with a *specific* rank.
345	
346	Axiom-clean: composes `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`
347	(Mathlib, gives `rank S Ω[S⁄R] = n`) with `Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free`
348	(Mathlib, transports module rank through `IsLocalizedModule`). The combination
349	provides the rank of the stalk-side Kähler differentials, which is one of the two
350	ingredients of the regularity conclusion at the stalk (the other ingredient is the
351	Stacks 00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula, still a Mathlib gap). -/
352	private theorem rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension
353	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
354	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra R S]
355	    (n : ℕ) [hS : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S]
356	    (M : Submonoid S)
357	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ]
358	    [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
359	    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
360	    Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n := by
361	  haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S := hS.isStandardSmooth
362	  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
363	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
364	    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M

641

646	  maximal ideal `m' ⊂ MvPolynomial ι k` has height `#ι`. This is built

648	  `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one` + `MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv`

652	  consumed via `ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`. The

655	  + `IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal`

660	the Step 1 named bridge (`ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`), and
661	the Step 3 quotient-form re-export
662	(`ringKrullDim_quotient_isRegular_eq_sub_length`) so the iter-201+ closure
663	collapses to building the Jacobian-regular-sequence witness alone. -/

665	/-- **Step 1 bridge (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200).** Ergonomic re-export of
666	`IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`: for a prime ideal `m ⊂ R` and
667	a localization `A = R_m`, `ringKrullDim A = m.height`. This is the boundary
668	translator inside `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` between
669	`Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` (the iter-183 CoheightBridge bridge,
670	phrased in `ringKrullDim`) and the Mathlib polynomial-ring height arithmetic

674	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime
675	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (m : Ideal R) [m.IsPrime]
676	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
677	    ringKrullDim A = m.height :=
678	  IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A

680	/-- **Step 2 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), `Fin n` form.** For a
681	field `k` and a maximal ideal `m ⊂ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`, the height of
682	`m` equals `n`.
683	
684	This is the substantive Step 2 ingredient: combined with the standard-smooth
685	presentation surjection `MvPolynomial (Fin (#ι)) k ↠ S`, it computes the
686	height of any contracted maximal ideal `m' = preimage(m) ⊂ MvPolynomial (Fin (#ι)) k`
687	explicitly. The closed-point case is the substantive one — every maximal ideal
688	of `MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` has height exactly `n` (independent of whether `k`
689	is algebraically closed; the Jacobson property suffices).
690	
691	Axiom-clean proof: induction on `n`. The base case `n = 0` is trivial (no
692	maximal-ideal content beyond `bot_le`). The inductive step transports `m` along
693	`MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv` to a maximal ideal `m'` of
694	`Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)`, contracts to `p = m' ∩ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`
695	(maximal by `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` since
696	`MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` is Jacobson), and applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`
697	followed by the inductive hypothesis on `p`. The upper bound follows from
698	`Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top` + `MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing`. -/
699	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_ge_card_of_field
700	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (n : ℕ) :
701	    ∀ (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) [m.IsMaximal], (n : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height := by
702	  induction n with
703	  | zero => intro m _; exact_mod_cast bot_le
704	  | succ n ih =>
705	    intro m _
706	    let e := MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv k n
707	    let m' : Ideal (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) := Ideal.map e.toRingEquiv m
708	    haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e.toRingEquiv)
709	    let p : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k) := Ideal.comap Polynomial.C m'
710	    haveI : p.IsMaximal := Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing m'
711	    haveI : m'.LiesOver p := ⟨rfl⟩
712	    have ih' := ih p
713	    have hh : m'.height = p.height + 1 := Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one p m'
714	    have hm_eq : m.height = m'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map e.toRingEquiv m).symm
715	    rw [hm_eq, hh, show ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (n : ℕ∞) + 1 from by push_cast; ring]
716	    gcongr
717	
718	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_le_natCard_of_field.{v}
719	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {ιx : Type v} [Finite ιx]
720	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) (hm : m ≠ ⊤) :
721	    m.height ≤ Nat.card ιx := by
722	  have h1 : (m.height : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim (MvPolynomial ιx k) :=
723	    Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top hm
724	  rw [MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing, ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_field,
725	    zero_add] at h1
726	  exact_mod_cast h1
727	
728	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card
729	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (n : ℕ)
730	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) [hmax : m.IsMaximal] :
731	    m.height = n := by
732	  apply le_antisymm
733	  · have := MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_le_natCard_of_field k (ιx := Fin n) m hmax.ne_top
734	    simpa using this
735	  · exact MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_ge_card_of_field k n m
736	
737	/-- **Step 2 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), general `[Finite ι]` form.**
738	For a field `k`, a finite index type `ι`, and a maximal ideal
739	`m ⊂ MvPolynomial ι k`, the height of `m` equals `Nat.card ι`.
740	
741	Axiom-clean: transports the `Fin n` form
742	`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card` along the canonical
743	`MvPolynomial.renameEquiv` induced by `(Finite.equivFin ι)`. -/
744	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard.{v}
745	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (ιx : Type v) [Finite ιx]
746	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) [m.IsMaximal] :
747	    m.height = Nat.card ιx := by
748	  classical
749	  haveI : Fintype ιx := Fintype.ofFinite ιx
750	  let σ : ιx ≃ Fin (Fintype.card ιx) := Fintype.equivFin ιx
751	  let e : MvPolynomial ιx k ≃+* MvPolynomial (Fin (Fintype.card ιx)) k :=
752	    (MvPolynomial.renameEquiv k σ).toRingEquiv
753	  let m' : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin (Fintype.card ιx)) k) := Ideal.map e m
754	  haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e)
755	  have hm_eq : m.height = m'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map e m).symm
756	  have hcard : Nat.card ιx = Fintype.card ιx := by simp [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]
757	  rw [hm_eq, hcard]
758	  exact MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card k (Fintype.card ιx) m'
759	
760	/-- **Step 1 + Step 2 capstone (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200).** For any
761	localisation `A` of `MvPolynomial ιx k` at a maximal ideal `m`, the Krull
762	dimension of `A` equals `Nat.card ιx`.
763	
764	This is the most direct project-side consumer route for Step 1 + Step 2: it
765	takes the polynomial-ring height equality (`m.height = Nat.card ιx` via
766	`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard`) and pre-composes with the
767	Step 1 bridge (`ringKrullDim A = m.height` via
768	`ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`) to expose the closed form
769	`ringKrullDim A = Nat.card ιx` without going through `Ideal.height`.
770	
771	Combined with Step 3 (`ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence`)
772	plus the Jacobian-regular-sequence witness (iter-201+ Lane COE), this yields
773	`ringKrullDim Sₘ = Nat.card ιx − rs.length` for the standard-smooth quotient
774	`Sₘ = A ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs`. -/
775	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial.{v}
776	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ιx : Type v} [Finite ιx]
777	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) [m.IsMaximal]
778	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra (MvPolynomial ιx k) A]
779	    [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
780	    ringKrullDim A = (Nat.card ιx : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
781	  rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A,
782	    MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard k ιx m]
783	  rfl
784	
785	/-- **Step 3 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), parameterised additive form.**
786	For a Noetherian local ring `R'` with `ringKrullDim R' = a` (a natural number) and
787	a regular sequence `rs : List R'` of length `b`, the Krull dimension of the
788	quotient `R' ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs` plus `b` equals `a`.
789	
790	This is the parameterised version of Mathlib's
791	`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular` packaged in `WithBot ℕ∞`
792	arithmetic, with the natural-number values of dimension and length explicit. It is
793	the additive form preferred over a subtraction form because `WithBot ℕ∞` does not
794	carry a subtraction structure.
795	
796	The consumer of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` chains:
797	
798	* `R'` = the polynomial-ring localisation `R'_{m'} = (MvPolynomial ι k)_{m'}`,
799	* `hDim` (a = #ι) = `MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard` composed with
800	  the Step 1 bridge `ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`,
801	* `hReg` = the Jacobian-criterion regular-sequence witness (residual Mathlib gap;
802	  iter-201+ Lane COE),
803	* `hLen` (b = #σ) = the cardinality of the regular sequence.
804	
805	Then `ringKrullDim Sₘ = a − b = n` follows by cancellation from this lemma.
806	
807	Axiom-clean: 3-line re-export. -/
808	private theorem ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence
809	    {R' : Type*} [CommRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R'] [IsLocalRing R']
810	    (rs : List R') (hReg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs)
811	    {a b : ℕ} (hDim : ringKrullDim R' = (a : WithBot ℕ∞))
812	    (hLen : rs.length = b) :
813	    ringKrullDim (R' ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (b : WithBot ℕ∞) = (a : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
814	  have h := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs hReg
815	  rw [hDim, hLen] at h
816	  exact h
817	
818	/-! ### Iter-201 Step A2 substrate (cotangent linear-independence transport)
819	
820	Two axiom-clean substrate helpers needed by the iter-201+ A2 step of the
821	Stacks-00SW Jacobian-regular-sequence witness construction
822	(per `analogies/coe-stacks00sw.md`). They package the
823	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.basisCotangent` linear-independence statement
824	in two forward-friendly forms:
825	
826	* `submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent` — the
827	  `S`-lin-indep version of the family `i ↦ Cotangent.mk ⟨P.relation i, _⟩`,
828	  obtained directly from `P.basisCotangent.linearIndependent` via
829	  `basisCotangent_apply`.
830	* `submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent_localized`
831	  — the same family transported through the canonical localisation map
832	  `Cot →ₗ[S] LocalizedModule p.primeCompl Cot` at any prime ideal `p` of `S`,
833	  yielding `Localization.AtPrime p`-lin-indep. This composes the previous
834	  helper with Mathlib's `LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule`.
835	
836	Together these realise Analogue D of the analogist recipe (lin-indep
837	preservation under localisation). The remaining substantive step in the A2
838	chain — identifying `LocalizedModule p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent`
839	with `(I·A) / (I·A)²` over the localisation `A = S_p`, i.e.\ the
840	conormal-localisation iso (Stacks 02JK, `Algebra.Generators.Cotangent.tensor`
841	variant for `AtPrime` rather than `Localization.Away`) — is a separate
842	Mathlib gap not landed here. -/
843	
844	/-- **Iter-201 Step A2 substrate, Mathlib gap-free part 1.** For a submersive
845	presentation `P : R → S` of an `R`-algebra, the family
846	`i ↦ Cotangent.mk ⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩`
847	in `P.toExtension.Cotangent` is `S`-linearly independent.
848	
849	This is a forward-ergonomics re-package of `P.basisCotangent.linearIndependent`
850	+ `P.basisCotangent_apply`: the basis-form lin-indep statement is restated
851	with the explicit `Cotangent.mk`-of-relation form on the consumer side, so
852	downstream callers do not need to manually unfold the basis abstraction.
853	
854	Axiom-clean: 1 `Basis.linearIndependent` + 1 funext rewrite. -/
855	private theorem submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent
856	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
857	    {ix sx : Type*} [Finite sx]
858	    (P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx) :
859	    LinearIndependent S
860	      (fun i : sx => Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.mk
861	        (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker)) := by
862	  have h := P.basisCotangent.linearIndependent
863	  have heq : (fun i : sx => Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.mk
864	        (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker)) = P.basisCotangent := by
865	    funext i
866	    exact (P.basisCotangent_apply i).symm
867	  rw [heq]
868	  exact h
869	
870	/-- **Iter-201 Step A2 substrate, Mathlib gap-free part 2.** Transport of
871	`submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent` through the
872	canonical localisation map of `P.toExtension.Cotangent` at any prime `p` of
873	`S`. The resulting family in `LocalizedModule p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent`
874	is `Localization.AtPrime p`-linearly independent.
875	
876	Composes the previous helper with Mathlib's
877	`LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule` (in
878	`Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Module`) and the canonical
879	`LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap` + `LocalizedModule.isModule`-driven
880	`IsLocalizedModule` instance on the generic `LocalizedModule` construction.
881	
882	This is Analogue D of the `coe-stacks00sw` mathlib-analogist recipe at the
883	level of the un-quotiented cotangent module; the residual A2 work
884	(identifying the localised cotangent with `(I·A)/(I·A)²` over `A = S_p`,
885	i.e.\ Stacks 02JK's conormal-localisation iso) remains a Mathlib gap and is
886	not landed here.
887	
888	Axiom-clean: 1 invocation of `LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule` on top
889	of `submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent`. -/
890	private theorem submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent_localized
891	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
892	    {ix sx : Type*} [Finite sx]
893	    (P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx)
894	    (p : Ideal S) [p.IsPrime] :
895	    LinearIndependent (Localization.AtPrime p)
896	      (fun i : sx =>
897	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent)
898	          (Algebra.Extension.Cotangent.mk
899	            (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker))) :=
900	  LinearIndependent.of_isLocalizedModule (Localization.AtPrime p) p.primeCompl
901	    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap p.primeCompl P.toExtension.Cotangent)
902	    (submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent P)
903	
904	/-- **Iter-201 Steps 1+2+3 composite (Mathlib-gap-conditional).** Given a
905	regular-sequence witness for a list `rs : List A` on a localisation `A` of
906	`MvPolynomial ix k` at a maximal ideal `m` (the substantive iter-201+ Step A
907	obligation, currently a Mathlib gap), the Krull dimension of the
908	quotient `A ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs` plus the length of `rs` equals
909	`Nat.card ix`.
910	
911	This is the direct composition of the iter-200 capstone
912	`ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial` (polynomial-ring side
913	Krull dimension) with the iter-200 additive form
914	`ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence` (regular-sequence
915	dimension drop), specialised to the polynomial-ring setting at a maximal
916	ideal. It gives the cleanest forward-compatible Step 3 "endpoint":
917	once the Step A `IsRegular` witness lands axiom-clean upstream (per the
918	`coe-stacks00sw` analogist recipe), this lemma reduces the
919	`ringKrullDim Sₘ = n` conclusion of Stacks 00OE to a one-line invocation.
920	
921	Axiom-clean: composes Steps 1+2 (iter-200) and Step 3 (iter-200) with the
922	trivial `IsLocalization.AtPrime.isLocalRing` instance. -/
923	private theorem ringKrullDim_quotient_localization_MvPolynomial_of_regular.{v}
924	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ix : Type v} [Finite ix]
925	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ix k)) [m.IsMaximal]
926	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra (MvPolynomial ix k) A]
927	    [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] [IsNoetherianRing A]
928	    (rs : List A) (hReg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular A rs)
929	    {b : ℕ} (hLen : rs.length = b) :
930	    ringKrullDim (A ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (b : WithBot ℕ∞) =
931	      (Nat.card ix : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
932	  haveI : IsLocalRing A := IsLocalization.AtPrime.isLocalRing A m
933	  exact ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence rs hReg
934	    (ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial m A) hLen
935	
936	/-- **Stage 6 sub-gap (i) resolution (iter-198).** Axiom-clean substrate helper:
937	every `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S` instance can be promoted to
938	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S` for some specific `n : ℕ`
939	(extracted from the underlying submersive presentation via
940	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`).
941	
942	This closes the iter-193 "sub-gap (i): relative-dimension determination"
943	identified in the docstring of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` below.
944	The remaining Stage 6 gap is solely (ii) the Stacks-00OE smooth-algebra
945	Krull-dimension formula plus the Stacks-02JK cotangent-Kähler bridge over a
946	field.
947	
948	Axiom-clean: a 4-line unpacking of `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.out` via
949	`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`. No
950	Mathlib gap; the only reason this was a sub-gap iter-191--194 was an
951	overconservative read of `IsStandardSmooth.relativeDimension`'s
952	`Classical.choice` apparatus. -/
953	private theorem exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_isStandardSmooth
954	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
955	    [hS : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S] :
956	    ∃ n : ℕ, Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S := by

996	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
997	    {n : ℕ} [h : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S] :

1002	/-- **Lane COE Step B.b (iter-202).** The affine-open stalk localisation: for an

1005	Re-export of `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`, decoupled here from

1007	`IsLocalization.AtPrime` instance without re-deriving the Stage-3 algebra package.

1009	theorem isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen

1011	    letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩

1013	  hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩

1015	/-- **Lane COE Step B.c helper (iter-202).** On a scheme whose underlying carrier
1016	is a subsingleton (the topological space has at most one point — e.g.\
1017	`Spec (.of k̄)` for a field `k̄`, which has a unique point by
1018	`PrimeSpectrum.instUnique`), every nonempty open subset equals `⊤`. Axiom-clean:
1019	the unique-point argument via `Subsingleton.elim`. -/
1020	private lemma open_eq_top_of_subsingleton {X : Scheme.{u}} [Subsingleton X]
1021	    (U : X.Opens) (hU : Nonempty U) : U = ⊤ := by
1022	  obtain ⟨⟨x, hx⟩⟩ := hU
1023	  ext y
1024	  simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_top, Set.mem_univ, iff_true]
1025	  rwa [Subsingleton.elim y x]

1027	/-- **Lane COE Step B.c (iter-202).** The `Γ(Spec (.of k̄), U) = k̄` bridge: for a
1028	field `k̄` and any nonempty open `U` of `Spec (.of k̄)`, the section ring over `U`
1029	is ring-isomorphic to `k̄`. Since `Spec (.of k̄)` has a subsingleton carrier
1030	(`PrimeSpectrum.instUnique`), `U = ⊤` and the sections collapse to the global
1031	sections `Γ(Spec (.of k̄), ⊤) ≅ k̄` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`). This supplies the
1032	algebraically-closed base ring of the standard-smooth presentation
1033	(`R = Γ(Spec (.of k̄), U)`) with a clean `k̄`-identification — the geometric input
1034	to the closed-point residue-field bridge `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`.
1035	Axiom-clean: `subst` of `open_eq_top_of_subsingleton` + `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
1036	noncomputable def gammaSpecField_ringEquiv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
1037	    (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (hU : Nonempty U) :
1038	    Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) ≃+* kbar := by
1039	  have h : U = ⊤ := open_eq_top_of_subsingleton U hU
1040	  subst h
1041	  exact (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv

1215	/-- **Step A1 — Matsumura's regular-sequence criterion (iter-203, Lane COE).**
1216	On a regular local Noetherian ring `(A, 𝔪)`, a finite sequence `f₁, …, f_c ∈ 𝔪`
1217	whose images in the cotangent space `𝔪/𝔪²` are `κ(A)`-linearly independent forms
1218	a `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular` sequence.
1219	
1220	This is Matsumura, *Commutative Ring Theory*, Thm 14.2 (cf. Stacks tag `00NQ`):
1221	linearly independent elements of `𝔪/𝔪²` in a regular local ring form a regular
1222	sequence (indeed they extend to a regular system of parameters). The proof is
1223	the iter-203 blueprint recipe `\subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200`, Step A1:
1224	induction on the length `c` (here `n`), peeling the head `f₁` via
1225	`isRegular_cons_of_quotient_ring`. At each step the head is a non-zero-divisor
1226	(`A` is a domain by `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal`, and
1227	`f₁ ≠ 0` since `f₁ ∉ 𝔪²`), the quotient `A ⧸ span{f₁}` is again regular local
1228	(`RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq`),
1229	and the tail's cotangent classes stay `κ`-linearly independent in the quotient
1230	(`matsumura_descent_cotangent`). The two
1231	`RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.*` inputs were promoted to public in iter-202.
1232	
1233	Project-local because Mathlib at commit `b80f227` ships neither this criterion
1234	nor the regular-local⟹domain / regular-local-quotient facts it consumes.
1235	Axiom-clean. -/
1236	private theorem matsumura_isRegular_of_linearIndependent_cotangent
1237	    {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [IsLocalRing A] [IsNoetherianRing A] [IsRegularLocalRing A]
1238	    (n : ℕ) (rs : Fin n → A) (hrs_mem : ∀ i, rs i ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A)
1239	    (h_lin : LinearIndependent (IsLocalRing.ResidueField A)
1240	               (fun i => (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).toCotangent ⟨rs i, hrs_mem i⟩)) :
1241	    RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular A (List.ofFn rs) := by
1242	  suffices H : ∀ (n : ℕ) (A : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsLocalRing A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1243	      [IsRegularLocalRing A] (rs : Fin n → A)
1244	      (hrs_mem : ∀ i, rs i ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A),
1245	      LinearIndependent (IsLocalRing.ResidueField A)
1246	        (fun i => (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).toCotangent ⟨rs i, hrs_mem i⟩) →
1247	      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular A (List.ofFn rs) from H n A rs hrs_mem h_lin
1248	  clear h_lin hrs_mem rs n
1249	  intro n
1250	  induction n with
1251	  | zero =>
1252	    intro A _ _ _ _ rs _ _
1253	    rw [List.ofFn_zero]; exact RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.nil A A
1254	  | succ m ih =>
1255	    intro A _ _ _ _ rs hmem hlin
1256	    have hxmem : rs 0 ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A := hmem 0
1257	    have hxne0 : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).toCotangent ⟨rs 0, hxmem⟩ ≠ 0 := hlin.ne_zero 0
1258	    have hxnotsq : rs 0 ∉ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A ^ 2 := fun hsq =>
1259	      hxne0 ((Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero _ ⟨rs 0, hxmem⟩).mpr hsq)
1260	    have hspan_pos : ∃ k, (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).spanFinrank = k + 1 := by
1261	      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos ((IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).spanFinrank) with h0 | hpos
1262	      · exfalso
1263	        have hfg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).FG := Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
1264	        have hbot : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A = ⊥ :=
1265	          (Submodule.spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot hfg).mp h0
1266	        apply hxnotsq
1267	        have hb : rs 0 ∈ (⊥ : Ideal A) := hbot ▸ hxmem
1268	        rw [Submodule.mem_bot] at hb; rw [hb]; exact Ideal.zero_mem _
1269	      · exact ⟨(IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A).spanFinrank - 1, by omega⟩
1270	    obtain ⟨k, hk⟩ := hspan_pos
1271	    obtain ⟨hNT, hLR, hRLR, _hdim_quot⟩ :=
1272	      RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq
1273	        hk (rs 0) hxmem hxnotsq
1274	    haveI : Nontrivial (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) := hNT
1275	    haveI : IsLocalRing (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) := hLR
1276	    haveI : IsRegularLocalRing (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) := hRLR
1277	    haveI : IsNoetherianRing (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) := inferInstance
1278	    have hg_mem : ∀ i : Fin m, (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) (rs i.succ))
1279	        ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) := by
1280	      intro i
1281	      have hmax : (Ideal.comap (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))
1282	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))).IsMaximal :=
1283	        Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
1284	      have hc : Ideal.comap (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))
1285	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))
1286	          = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A := (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff A).mp hmax
1287	      have hin : rs i.succ ∈ Ideal.comap (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))
1288	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A))) := by
1289	        rw [hc]; exact hmem i.succ
1290	      exact Ideal.mem_comap.mp hin
1291	    have hlin' := matsumura_descent_cotangent m rs hmem hlin hg_mem
1292	    have hih := ih (A ⧸ Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A))
1293	      (fun i => Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) (rs i.succ)) hg_mem hlin'
1294	    haveI : IsDomain A := RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal A
1295	    have hxne0' : rs 0 ≠ 0 := fun h => hxnotsq (by rw [h]; exact Ideal.zero_mem _)
1296	    have h1 : IsSMulRegular A (rs 0) := IsSMulRegular.of_ne_zero hxne0'
1297	    rw [List.ofFn_succ]
1298	    apply isRegular_cons_of_quotient_ring h1
1299	    have hmapeq : (List.ofFn (fun i : Fin m => rs i.succ)).map
1300	        (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)))
1301	        = List.ofFn (fun i => Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span ({rs 0} : Set A)) (rs i.succ)) := by
1302	      rw [List.map_ofFn]; rfl
1303	    rw [hmapeq]; exact hih

1305	/-- **Stacks `00TT`, Jacobian-criterion direction (regularity conclusion).**
1306	For a smooth integral variety `X` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, the
1307	stalk of `X` at every point is a regular local ring.
1308	
1309	This is the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00TT` (Lemma 10.140.3
1310	"Smooth algebras over fields"): given an affine chart `Spec A ⊆ X`, the
1311	\(\bar k\)-algebra `A` is smooth at every prime `𝔮`, and the localisation
1312	`A_𝔮 = X.presheaf.stalk z` (for `z ∈ X` lying over `𝔮`) is regular by the
1313	"in this case the local ring `A_𝔮` is regular" clause of the cited lemma.
1314

1527	/-- **Helper for `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`.** On a smooth integral variety
1528	over an algebraically closed field, the stalk at a codimension-`1` point has a
1529	*principal nonzero* maximal ideal. Encodes the substantive geometric content
1530	of "regular in codimension one": smoothness ⟹ `IsRegularLocalRing` at the
1531	stalk (Stacks `00PD`/`00TT`), and a Noetherian regular local ring with
1532	`ringKrullDim = 1` has principal nonzero maximal ideal (Stacks `02IZ`),
1533	via the cotangent-space `finrank = 1` characterisation.
1534	
1535	**Iter-187 Lane M↓ refactor.** The previous body bundled both Mathlib gaps —
1536	smooth ⟹ regular (Stacks 00TT) and codim-1 ⟹ Krull-dim-1 — into an inline
1537	`hreg_dim` conjunction whose first conjunct was a bare `sorry`. The Krull-dim
1538	half has been closed axiom-clean since iter-184 via
1539	`Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` (the iter-183 `CoheightBridge.lean`
1540	bridge); the iter-187 refactor isolates the remaining Stacks-00TT gap as the
1541	named helper `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` above. This helper is now
1542	*axiom-clean modulo the named Stacks-00TT gap*: its body discharges every
1543	conclusion from explicit Mathlib lemmas and the named regularity helper.
1544	Once `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` closes upstream the whole chain
1545	becomes axiom-clean automatically. -/
1546	private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
1547	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1548	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
1549	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1550	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1551	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1552	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
1553	    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
1554	      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
1555	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk (needed for the cotangent-space API):
1556	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the automatic `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
1557	  -- through to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk picks up
1558	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance).
1559	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
1560	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
1561	  -- Regularity half: extracted to the named helper above
1562	  -- (`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`), which captures the Stacks-00TT
1563	  -- Jacobian-criterion gap as a narrow named typed sorry. No further
1564	  -- inline `sorry` is required at this step.
1565	  have hreg : IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
1566	    isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth X z
1567	  -- Krull-dim half: closes via the iter-183 axiom-clean CoheightBridge
1568	  -- equality + the codim-1 witness `_hz`.
1569	  have hdim : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1 := by
1570	    rw [Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight]
1571	    exact_mod_cast _hz
1572	  -- From regularity + dim = 1, the cotangent space has `finrank = 1`
1573	  -- (Mathlib `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
1574	  have hfin :
1575	      Module.finrank
1576	        (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z))
1577	        (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) = 1 := by
1578	    have h := (IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace _).mp hreg
1579	    rw [hdim] at h
1580	    exact_mod_cast h
1581	  -- Principal maximal ideal: `finrank ≤ 1 ↔ IsPrincipal maximalIdeal`
1582	  -- (Mathlib `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff`).
1583	  have hprin : Submodule.IsPrincipal
1584	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
1585	    IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff.mp hfin.le
1586	  -- `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`: equivalent to "not a field" by Mathlib
1587	  -- `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq`, and a field has Krull dim 0,
1588	  -- contradicting `hdim : ringKrullDim = 1` via `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField`.
1589	  have hne : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
1590	    intro hbot
1591	    have hF : IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
1592	      IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr hbot
1593	    have h0 : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 0 :=
1594	      ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField hF
1595	    -- Contradiction: `1 = 0` in `WithBot ℕ∞`.
1596	    rw [hdim] at h0
1597	    exact_mod_cast h0
1598	  exact ⟨hprin, hne⟩
1599	
1600	/-- **Smooth + codim-1 ⇒ DVR.** For a nonsingular integral variety `X` over
1601	an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a point `η : X.left` with
1602	`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
1603	valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).
1604	
1605	The proof reduces to "regular local ring of Krull dimension `1` is a DVR"
1606	(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
1607	plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
1608	regular of dimension `1`. The geometric step is packaged in the private helper
1609	`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`; the rest assembles the
1610	DVR instance via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`.
1611	
1612	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;
1613	Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence). -/
1614	theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
1615	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1616	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1617	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1618	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1619	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1620	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
1621	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1622	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
1623	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
1624	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
1625	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
1626	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
1627	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
1628	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
1629	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
1630	  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
1631	  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
1632	    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz
1633	  -- The stalk is a local Noetherian domain with principal maximal ideal:
1634	  -- promote `IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal)` to a `IsPrincipalIdealRing` instance
1635	  -- and conclude DVR via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk`.
1636	  -- The TFAE entry "principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR" handles the conversion
1637	  -- once we know `¬ IsField` (which follows from `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`).
1638	  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1639	    intro hF
1640	    exact hne ((IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq).mp hF)
1641	  have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
1642	  -- TFAE entry index 4 is `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)`.
1643	  exact ((tfae.out 0 4).mpr hprin)
