1647	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
1648	
1649	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
1650	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
1651	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
1652	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
1653	
1654	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
1655	
1656	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
1657	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
1658	
1659	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
1660	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
1661	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
1662	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
1663	
1664	The proof combines two steps:
1665	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
1666	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
1667	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
1668	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
1669	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
1670	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
1671	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
1672	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
1673	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
1674	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
1675	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
1676	
1677	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1678	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
1679	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
1680	
1681	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
1682	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
1683	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
1684	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1685	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1686	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1687	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1688	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1689	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1690	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
1691	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1692	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1693	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
1694	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
1695	  -- Derived structural instance: the target Y.left is a separated scheme
1696	  -- (as `Y.hom : Y.left ⟶ Spec kbar` is separated and `Spec kbar` is affine
1697	  -- hence separated over the terminal).
1698	  haveI : Y.left.IsSeparated := by
1699	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
1700	    have heq : terminal.from Y.left = Y.hom ≫ terminal.from _ := Subsingleton.elim _ _
1701	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
1702	  -- The substantive Milne 3.1 proof body reduces to showing Dom(f) = ⊤:
1703	  --
1704	  -- * Step 1 (codim-1 components of Z(f) ruled out) uses the valuative
1705	  --   criterion of properness on the complete target Y plus the DVR-stalk
1706	  --   structure at codim-1 points (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` above, gated
1707	  --   on `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6 Mathlib gap).
1708	  -- * Step 2 (codim-≥2 extension) uses the depth-≥2 local-cohomology
1709	  --   vanishing `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point (Stacks 0AVF), a
1710	  --   genuine Mathlib gap at commit `b80f227`. The codim-1-free hypothesis
1711	  --   `_hf` alone suffices for Step 1's conclusion but Step 2 requires the
1712	  --   local-cohomology / `Module.depth` bridge from
1713	  --   `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`).
1714	  --
1715	  -- Once `Dom(f) = ⊤` is established, the unique morphism extension is the
1716	  -- composition `X.left.topIso.inv ≫ (X.left.isoOfEq h).inv ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom`;
1717	  -- uniqueness follows from the reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1718	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` / `PartialMap.equiv_iff_of_isSeparated`).
1719	  --
1720	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: this lane is gated on Stacks 0AVF
1721	  -- (depth-≥2 H¹ vanishing) + `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6.
1722	  sorry

1724	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1725	
1726	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1727	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1728	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
1729	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
1730	
1731	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1732	
1733	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1734	codim 1.**
1735	
1736	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1737	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1738	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1739	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
1740	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
1741	
1742	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1743	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1744	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1745	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1746	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1747	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1748	
1749	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1750	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1751	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1752	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1753	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1754	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1755	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1756	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1757	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1758	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
1759	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1760	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1761	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
1762	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1763	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1764	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
1765	  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the
1766	  -- detailed chain (TBD). Each substep is project-side buildable on top of
1767	  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4
1768	  -- (pole-divisor intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1) is the
1769	  -- substantive Mathlib gap.
1770	  --
1771	  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
1772	  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`, dense on `U × U` for
1773	  --   any representative `(U, φ_U) ∈ f`. Needs the binary product on
1774	  --   `GrpObj`-objects and the right-monoidal-cat structure used in this
1775	  --   project (`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, `MonObj.mul`).
1776	  -- * Substep 2: `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ↔ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the formula
1777	  --   `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
1778	  --   neighbourhood — direct from the group-law continuity + openness of
1779	  --   the domain of definition.
1780	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1781	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1782	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
1783	  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
1784	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1785	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
1786	  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
1787	  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
1788	  --   This is the substantive Mathlib gap: Mathlib's
1789	  --   `RamificationTheory.PrincipalIdeal` provides Krull's HPP for ideals
1790	  --   but the scheme-level codim-1 intersection-with-diagonal lemma is
1791	  --   not packaged.
1792	  --
1793	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: gated on (a) the function-field-pullback bridge for
1794	  -- `Scheme.RationalMap` (also blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`),
1795	  -- (b) the codim-1 pole-divisor / diagonal intersection lemma (Hartshorne
1796	  -- AG 9.2 scheme-level form).
1797	  sorry

1799	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
1800	
1801	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
1802	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
1803	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
1804	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
1805	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
1806	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
1807	witnesses are constructed in this project).
1808	
1809	### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)
1810	
1811	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
1812	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
1813	
1814	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
1815	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
1816	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
1817	
1818	which requires the pullback machinery
1819	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
1820	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
1821	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
1822	`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
1823	at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
1824	a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
1825	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
1826	
1827	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
1828	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
1829	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
1830	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
1831	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
1832	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
1833	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
1834	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
1835	the pullback machinery lands.
1836	
1837	Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
1838	of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/
1839	
1840	/-- **Definedness at the generic point of a prime divisor, repackaged.**
1841	
1842	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
1843	`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor`, the following
1844	are equivalent:
1845	
1846	1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e.\ `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
1847	2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
1848	   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
1849	   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.
1850	
1851	This is the canonical existential characterisation of `f.domain` membership
1852	via `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`, with the two
1853	conjuncts under the existential reordered so the `toRationalMap = f`
1854	component lands first (matching the way later witnesses in this project
1855	construct partial-map representatives).
1856	
1857	**Iter-179 Lane D scope.** This statement is the auditor-178B Path D2 honest
1858	fallback: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 "regular = no pole" Weil-divisor
1859	reformulation (Stacks `02ME`), which would tighten the right-hand side to
1860	an `ord_W ≥ 0` condition on every pulled-back regular function, is deferred
1861	to a future lemma that builds the missing `RationalMap → function-field`
1862	pullback machinery. See the section-level docstring above. -/
1863	theorem mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point
1864	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1865	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1866	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1867	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1868	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1869	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1870	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1871	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1872	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (W : X.left.PrimeDivisor) :
1873	    W.point ∈ f.domain ↔
1874	      ∃ (g : X.left.PartialMap Y.left),
1875	        g.toRationalMap = f ∧ W.point ∈ (g.domain : Set X.left) := by
1876	  rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]
1877	  exact ⟨fun ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩ => ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩, fun ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩ => ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩⟩
1878	
1879	end RationalMap
1880	
1881	end Scheme
1882	
1883	end AlgebraicGeometry

10	# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
11	
12	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
14	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
15	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
16	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
17	
18	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
19	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
20	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
21	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
22	
23	1. a binary tensor-product operation
24	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
25	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
26	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
27	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.
28	
29	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
30	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
31	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
32	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
33	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
34	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
35	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.
36

100	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
101	
102	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
103	
104	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
105	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
106	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
107	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
108	
109	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: the
110	body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body lifts
111	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
112	the small Zariski site of `X`. -/
113	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
114	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
115	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
116	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
117	
118	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
119	
120	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
121	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
122	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
123	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
124	
125	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. iter-202 Lane TS
126	scaffold: the body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body inherits the
127	morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
128	sheafification. -/
129	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
130	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
131	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
132	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
133	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
134	
135	/-! ## §2. The monoidal-category structure -/
136	
137	/-- **Monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
138	
139	The bifunctor `⊗_X` of `tensorObj_functoriality`, with unit object the
140	structure sheaf `O_X`, associator `α`, left/right unitors `λ`, `ρ`, and
141	braiding `β` inherited from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal` under sheafification,
142	satisfies the pentagon, triangle, and hexagon axioms; hence `Scheme.Modules X`
143	carries a canonical (symmetric) monoidal-category structure.
144	
145	Per blueprint `thm:scheme_modules_monoidal`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: the
146	body is a typed `sorry`; the iter-203+ body discharges the coherence axioms
147	affine-locally (each reduces to the analogous identity for the tensor product
148	of modules over a commutative ring) and assembles the
149	`CategoryTheory.MonoidalCategory` instance. -/
150	noncomputable instance monoidalCategory {X : Scheme.{u}} :
151	    MonoidalCategory (X.Modules) :=
152	  sorry
153	
154	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
155	
156	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
157	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
158	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
159	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
160	
161	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
162	
163	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
164	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
165	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: typed
166	`sorry`; the iter-203+ body trivialises both factors on a common affine cover
167	and uses that the tensor product of two free rank-one modules is free of rank
168	one. -/
169	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
170	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
171	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) :=
172	  sorry
173	
174	/-- **Inverse of an invertible module.**
175	
176	Every line bundle `L : X.Modules` has a two-sided tensor inverse: there is a
177	locally-trivial `Linv : X.Modules` (the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)`) together with
178	a tensor isomorphism `L ⊗_X Linv ≅ 𝟙_ (X.Modules)`. Per blueprint
179	`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: typed `sorry`;
180	the iter-203+ body builds the dual and the contraction isomorphism, which is an
181	isomorphism affine-locally on a trivialising cover. -/
182	lemma exists_tensorObj_inverse {X : Scheme.{u}} {L : X.Modules}
183	    (hL : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial L) :
184	    ∃ Linv : X.Modules, LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial Linv ∧
185	      Nonempty (tensorObj L Linv ≅ 𝟙_ (X.Modules)) :=
186	  sorry
187	
188	/-- **Restriction of `⊗` to the `LineBundle.OnProduct` carrier.**
189	
190	For `S`-schemes `C, T`, the bifunctor `⊗_{C ×_S T}` restricts to the subtype
191	`LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT` of locally-trivial modules on `C ×_S T`, with unit
192	the structure sheaf and the dual as two-sided inverse. Per blueprint
193	`lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct`. iter-202 Lane TS scaffold: typed `sorry`. -/
194	noncomputable def tensorObjOnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S)
195	    (L L' : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT :=
196	  ⟨tensorObj L.carrier L'.carrier,
197	    tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial L.isLocallyTrivial L'.isLocallyTrivial⟩
198	
199	end Modules
200

203	namespace PicSharp

205	/-- **Abelian-group structure on the relative Picard quotient via

207	
208	For a base scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, and a test object
209	`πT : T ⟶ S`, the relative Picard quotient
210	`Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) = Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`

214	
215	Per blueprint `thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj`. iter-202 Lane TS

219	`instance`) to avoid an instance diamond with the existing typed-`sorry`
220	`PicSharp.addCommGroup` instance in `RelPicFunctor.lean`. -/
221	noncomputable def addCommGroup_via_tensorObj {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
222	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
223	    AddCommGroup (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) :=

23	`ModuleCat R`), as well as the regular-sequence definition
24	`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`. The depth function `Module.depth`, the

47	1. `RingTheory.Module.depth` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the `I`-depth of a

63	   projective dimension over a Noetherian local ring.

112	/-! ## §1. Depth of a finite module
113	
114	The `I`-depth of a finite `R`-module `M` is the supremum in
115	`{0, 1, 2, …, ∞}` of the lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in `I`
116	(provided `IM ≠ M`; if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ∞`). Mathlib `b80f227`
117	exposes the regular-sequence predicate `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`
118	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence`) but not the resulting
119	numeric depth function — that is the gap this declaration fills.
120	
121	Blueprint reference: `def:depth` (Stacks tag 00LF). -/
122	
123	/-- The **`I`-depth** of a finite `R`-module `M`: the supremum (in `ℕ∞`) of
124	lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in the ideal `I`.
125	
126	When `IM = M` (the "trivial-quotient" case, e.g. `M = 0` or `I = R`) the
127	supremum is taken to be `⊤` by convention. When `(R, 𝔪)` is local one usually
128	calls `depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M` simply *the depth* of `M`.
129	
130	iter-176+: the body is the supremum
131	```
132	sSup { (n : ℕ∞) | ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ I) ∧
133	                  RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs }
134	```
135	folded with the `IM = M` clause. The signature is non-tautological: it
136	asserts a function `(Ideal R) → (M : Type v) → ℕ∞` matching the Stacks
137	00LF definition.
138	
139	**iter-179 Mathlib-gap check (Lane F Target 2 STRETCH)**: a pinned-commit
140	audit of `Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.Depth` (the only file under
141	`Mathlib/RingTheory/*` containing the word "depth") confirms that Mathlib
142	`b80f227` ships only depth-zero lemmas (`IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff`)
143	and *not* the numeric depth function itself. The one-liner re-export route
144	is therefore not available; the body stays a typed `sorry` until an
145	iter-180+ body lane fills the supremum-with-`IM=M` clause directly. -/
146	noncomputable def depth {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (_I : Ideal R)
147	    (_M : Type v) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : ℕ∞ :=
148	  open Classical in
149	  if _I • (⊤ : Submodule R _M) = ⊤ then (⊤ : ℕ∞)
150	  else sSup { n : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = n ∧
151	    (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ _I) ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular _M rs }
152	
153	end Module

196	/-! ## §3. Depth via Ext characterisation
197	
198	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a
199	nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the depth of `M` equals the smallest index `i`
200	at which `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero (Stacks tag 00LP).
201	
202	This is the lemma A.4.a's downstream consumer ultimately reads off:
203	combined with the regular-sequence definition (`depth`), the Ext
204	characterisation provides both the *lower bound* (regular sequences exhibit
205	`Ext^i = 0` for `i < length(rs)`) and the *upper bound* (failure of any
206	extension lifts a non-zero element in `Ext^{depth}(κ, M)`).
207	
208	The signature pins the equivalence via the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`-vanishing-
209	below: `n ≤ depth(M) ↔ Ext^i(κ, M) = 0 for all i < n`. This is logically
210	equivalent to "depth(M) = smallest i with Ext^i ≠ 0" and is the form most
211	convenient for inductive proofs (Stacks 00LP proof: pick `x ∈ 𝔪`
212	non-zero-divisor, use long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` on `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0`).
213	
214	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_via_ext` (Stacks tag 00LP). -/

217	
218	For any `R`-modules `N, M` and any `x : R` in the annihilator of `N`, the
219	R-action `x • e` on `e : Ext^i_R(N, M)` is zero.
220	
221	Proof sketch: `x • e = (mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e (zero_add i)` (by R-linearity:
222	`mk₀_smul + smul_comp + mk₀_id_comp`). For `x ∈ Ann(N)` the morphism
223	`x • 𝟙_N : N ⟶ N` is the zero map, so `mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N) = mk₀ 0 = 0`
224	(`mk₀_zero`), and `0.comp e = 0` (`zero_comp`).
225

227	Stacks-00LP "`x ∈ 𝔪` annihilates `Ext^*(κ, -)`" trick, lifted to the more
228	general `x ∈ Ann(N)` form so it covers both `N = κ` and `N = R/(x_1,…,x_k)`. -/
229	private lemma ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator
230	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
231	    {N M : ModuleCat.{u} R} {i : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N M i)
232	    {x : R} (hx : x ∈ Module.annihilator R (N : Type u)) :
233	    x • e = 0 := by
234	  -- Step 1: x • 𝟙_N = 0 in ModuleCat (the underlying linear map sends m ↦ x • m,
235	  -- which is 0 since x ∈ Ann(N)).
236	  have hkill : (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N)) = (0 : N ⟶ N) := by
237	    apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
238	    apply LinearMap.ext
239	    intro n
240	    change x • n = 0
241	    exact Module.mem_annihilator.mp hx n
242	  -- Step 2: x • e = (mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e (zero_add i) by R-linearity.
243	  have hreflect :
244	      (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))).comp e (zero_add i)
245	        = x • e := by
246	    have hmk : (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))
247	                : Abelian.Ext.{u} N N 0)
248	        = x • CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (𝟙 N) :=
249	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R) x (𝟙 N)
250	    rw [hmk, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_comp,
251	        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp]
252	  -- Step 3: substitute hkill to collapse mk₀ … to mk₀ 0 = 0, then zero_comp.
253	  rw [← hreflect, hkill, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_zero,
254	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]

21	implies live **upstream** in `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma`, which is imported here. That

9	/-!
10	# The Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (A.4.d)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.d** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages Milne's
14	*Abelian Varieties* §III.6 Proposition 6.1: for a complete nonsingular curve
15	`C` of genus `g > 0` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked
16	`k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the Abel–Jacobi morphism
17	`ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` is the **Albanese morphism** of the pointed curve
18	`(C, P₀)`: any pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety with
19	`φ(P₀) = η_A` factors uniquely as `φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}` for a homomorphism of
20	group schemes `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.
21	
22	The chapter commits to the **symmetric-power route** (cube-free): the
23	symmetric assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` factors through
24	`Sym^g C`, the resulting morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` descends through
25	the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` to a rational map
26	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, and Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (`extend_to_av` in the sibling
27	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c) promotes it to a regular
28	morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The group-homomorphism property of `ψ` then
29	follows from `ψ(η_J) = η_A` together with Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a
30	consequence of `thm:rigidity_lemma`).
31	
32	## Status (iter-177 Lane 7 file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-177 Lane 7** file-skeleton. Each of the six
35	blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive type
36	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
38	
39	The 6 pinned declarations are:
40	
41	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.abelJacobi` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the
42	   regular morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
43	   `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`. Encoded against the file-internal
44	   placeholder `Pic0.jacobianScheme` for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (the A.3 upstream
45	   has not yet been split out; see §0 below).
46	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.SymmetricPower` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) —
47	   the `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C` of the curve `C`, as a `k̄`-scheme.
48	   Body gated on iter-178+ `SymmetricPower.lean` substrate (Milne III.3
49	   Proposition 3.1; Mumford 1970 II.7 / III.11 affine-and-glue recipe).
50	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerAVMap` (noncomputable def, ~5 LOC) —
51	   the symmetrised morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` produced by
52	   `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` from an input `φ : C ⟶ A`.
53	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerToJacobian` (noncomputable def,
54	   ~5 LOC) — the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
55	   `lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`.
56	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.descentThroughBirationalSigma` (theorem,
57	   ~10 LOC) — the descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism

59	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.albanese_universal_property` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
60	   the headline UP theorem (Milne Proposition III.6.1).

9	/-!
10	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
11	variety extends (A.4.c)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
14	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
15	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
16	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
17	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
18	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
19	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
20	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
21	
22	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension
23	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
24	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
25	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
26	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
27	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
28	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
29	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
30	defined.
31	
32	## Status (iter-175 Lane J file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-175 Lane J** file-skeleton: the single pinned
35	declaration carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the
36	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50) with a `sorry`
38	body. The body is iter-176+ work, gated on the sibling chapters
39	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (A.4.a) and
40	`Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b) landing their substantive
41	statements.
42	
43	The 1 pinned declaration is:
44	
45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
46	   — Milne Theorem 3.2: for `X` a smooth (= nonsingular) variety over an
47	   algebraically closed field `k̄`, `A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, and
48	   `f : X ⇢ A` a rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
49	   `g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
50	
51	## Note on type expressivity
52	
53	The signature is encoded against Mathlib's existing
54	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` (a partial-map equivalence class on
55	dense opens) and `Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap` (the canonical "regular morphism
56	yields a rational map" coercion). The substantive claim is the existence
57	and uniqueness of a `g : X.left ⟶ A.left` with `g.toRationalMap = f`. The
58	type is non-tautological: the existence is the content of Milne 3.2;
59	uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
60	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`).

13	# The Riemann–Roch formula in genus zero (RR.2)
14	
15	This file is the **RR.2** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
16	headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically
17	irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
18	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
19	
20	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ↦ `ℙ¹` classification routes
21	through:
22	
23	- `RR.1` (`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` / `WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil
24	  divisor group `Div(C)` and the degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`.
25	- **`RR.2` (this file, `RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`)**: the Riemann–Roch
26	  dimension formula `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` in genus `0` (with `deg D ≥ 0`),
27	  via the auxiliary Euler-characteristic identity
28	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g`.
29	- `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, future): the invertible sheaf
30	  `𝒪_C(D)`, the linear-equivalence isomorphism `𝒪_C(D) ≅ 𝒪_C(D')` for
31	  `D ∼ D'`, and the `H¹`-vanishing input
32	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(D)) = 0` for `deg D ≥ 0` on a genus-`0` curve.
33	- `RR.4` (`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`, future): the "two-section
34	  ⇒ `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` ⇒ `≅ ℙ¹`" classification.
35

8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`; the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all
17	project from the witness directly.
18	
19	This file currently contains TWO `sorry`-bodied declarations (the Phase-C scaffolding
20	inventory):
21	
22	1. `genusZeroWitness` (iter-127 scaffold): a `JacobianWitness C` for a genus-`0` curve,
23	   with underlying scheme `Spec k`. The body is a `sorry` to be closed once pieces
24	   (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land (iter-138+); the
25	   substantive mathematical content is the genus-`0` rigidity statement
26	   `rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`).
27	2. `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Phase-C OFF-LIMITS sorry): existence of an Albanese
28	   witness for an arbitrary smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve; gated on
29	   M2 + M3 closure (Albanese existence in genus `≥ 1` plus genus-`0` rigidity).
30	
31	The file contains:
32	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
33	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
34	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
35	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
36	- `genusZeroWitness`: an Albanese witness for a genus-`0` curve, body `sorry`
37	  (iter-127 scaffold; closure deferred to iter-138+).
38	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: existence of an Albanese witness for every curve —
39	  the headline Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and
40	  genus-`0` rigidity.
41	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
42	  Albanese witness.
43	
44	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
45	
46	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
47	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
48	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
49	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
50	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
51	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
52	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
53	-/

10	/-!
11	# Vanishing of `H¹` for skyscraper sheaves on a curve (RR.2.H¹)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.2.H¹** project-side build of the closed-point
14	skyscraper `H¹`-vanishing identity
15	
16	  `dim_{k̄} H¹(C, skyscraperSheaf P (ModuleCat.of k̄ k̄)) = 0`
17	
18	on a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`. The file is
19	the iter-191 Lane H **file-skeleton**: each of the eight pinned
20	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching
21	the blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/RiemannRoch_H1Vanishing.tex`),
22	with `sorry` bodies; the iter-192+ closure follows the classical
23	Hartshorne III.2.5 flasque-vanishing argument.
24	
25	The chapter strategy (Hartshorne III §2): an injective resolution of a
26	flasque sheaf yields a flasque quotient at each stage; the
27	global-sections functor is exact on a flasque-to-flasque-to-flasque
28	short exact sequence; hence the right-derived global-sections functor
29	vanishes on a flasque input in positive degree. We specialise the
30	abstract flasque-vanishing to the closed-point skyscraper sheaf, which
31	is flasque because it is the pushforward, along the closed embedding of
32	a one-point subspace, of the constant sheaf on an irreducible base.
33

37	   `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`.

39	   flasque sheaf is flasque (Hartshorne II.1, Ex. 1.16(d)).

41	   constant sheaf on an irreducible space is flasque (Hartshorne
42	   II.1, Ex. 1.16(a)).
43	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule_flasque_eq_zero` — flasque sheaves
44	   have zero `HModule` in positive degree (Hartshorne III.2.5).
45	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward_const` —
46	   skyscraper sheaf is the pushforward of a constant sheaf along the
47	   closed embedding of the closure of the support point.
48	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PrimeDivisor.closure_isIrreducible` — the
49	   closure of the support point of a `PrimeDivisor` is irreducible.

9	/-!
10	# Genus of a smooth proper curve
11	
12	The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.
13	
14	## Status (closed iteration 011 — `genus`)
15	
16	The iter-011 refactor added the `noncomputable` modifier (user-authorised
17	`2026-05-07`) and an import of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`
18	so the body uses the project's `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology
19	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009) of the structure sheaf
20	`Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` (iter-006). The body is the probe-confirmed
21	one-liner
22	
23	    Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
24	
25	which is the honest mathematical definition `dim_k H^1(C, O_C)`.
26	
27	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` for the informal proof sketch and
28	`.archon/STRATEGY.md` for the multi-phase build-out plan.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
40	noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
41	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
42	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
43	  Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
44	
45	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# Mumford Rigidity Lemma and its Milne §I.1 corollaries
10	
11	This file is the **abstract rigidity foundation** of the project's genus-`0` route. It hosts the
12	proven, axiom-clean **Mumford Rigidity Lemma (Form I)** and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries it
13	implies — the additive decomposition of a morphism out of a product (Cor 1.5) and the fact that
14	a pointed regular map of abelian varieties is a homomorphism (Cor 1.2).
15	
16	The genus-`0` final assembly (the `ℙ¹`-scaling shortcut, the headline
17	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme`, the Riemann–Roch bridge `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`) lives
18	downstream in `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`, which imports this file.
19	
20	## Contents
21	
22	The Rigidity-Lemma chain (PROVEN axiom-clean, iters 157–162; cube-free, cohomology-free, char-free):
23	
24	* `rigidity_snd_lift` — the cartesian-monoidal skeleton step of the Rigidity Lemma.
25	* `snd_left_isClosedMap` — bridge 1: completeness of `X` makes `snd : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Y` a closed map.
26	* `morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints` — the dense-closed-points hom-extensionality connective
27	  (route B's Step 2).
28	* `eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral` — the deep algebra of bridge 2 / Step 1: a proper
29	  integral `k̄`-scheme mapping to an affine is constant on `k̄`-points.
30	* `isIntegral_of_retract` — integrality descends to a retract; closes the slice/section
31	  assembly of route B's Step 1.
32	* `rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine` — route B Step 1 assembled.
33	* `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine` — bridge 2 (slice-constancy) end to end.
34	* `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open` — the genuine geometric content: a non-empty open of agreement.
35	* `rigidity_core` — the geometric core `f = retract ≫ f`.
36	* `rigidity_lemma` — **Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I).** The headline.
37	
38	The Milne §I.1 corollaries (both PROVEN axiom-clean from `rigidity_lemma`):
39	
40	* `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` — Corollary 1.5 (additive decomposition over a product).
41	* `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` — Corollary 1.2 (a pointed regular map of abelian varieties
42	  is a homomorphism).
43	
44	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
45	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §4; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Cor 1.2, Cor 1.5).
46	-/

15	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
16	
17	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
18	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
19	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
20	`Ω` on affine opens.
21	
22	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
23	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
24	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
25	
26	## References
27	
28	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
38	
39	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
40	
41	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
42	
43	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
44	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
45	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
46	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
47	
48	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
49	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
50	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
